description_long: This is a stone funerary monument resembling a sarcophagus or chest tomb, constructed from weathered gray stone with a pitched, gabled lid. The structure is supported by four decorative columns at each corner, each with ornate capitals and bases, and the main body features recessed panels with engraved inscriptions. The monument sits atop a multi-tiered rectangular base, with two broad steps leading up to the main plinth. The stone surface displays natural discoloration and patina, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The overall design is classical, with subtle ornamental details and a solid, imposing presence suitable for a cemetery or memorial park.
description: A weathered stone funerary monument with a gabled lid, decorative columns, and engraved panels, set on a multi-tiered rectangular base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main inscription panel, two front columns, and the broad steps of the base.
description_view_1: Front-right angled view highlighting the depth of the base, the right-side columns, and the sloped lid.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back panel, rear columns, and the full width of the base.
description_view_3: Rear-left angled view showing the left-side columns, rear inscription panel, and the monument's layered base.
attribute_inscription: There are engraved inscriptions on the front and rear panels, featuring block capital letters. The text is partially legible, likely in English, and appears to list names and dates.
attribute_ornamentation: Decorative columns with ornate capitals at each corner and subtle molding details along the lid and base.
attribute_base_design: The base is a multi-tiered rectangular platform with two broad steps leading up to the main plinth. The stone is solid and features simple, classical molding.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is a pitched, gabled stone lid with a central ridge, smooth surfaces, and no additional ornamentation.
